_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The California Regulatory Notice Register does not itself create registration or reporting obligations for private freight operators. Rather, it is the upstream publication mechanism through which California state agencies announce proposed rulemakings that may subsequently impose such obligations once finalized.

For the January 2024 registers specifically, no direct producer/EPR (Extended Producer Responsibility) registration scheme is announced on the face of the published table of contents. The notices most likely to produce downstream registration or reporting obligations for freight operations are:

**FPPC Conflict-of-Interest Code amendments (Notice File No. Z2024-0109-07):** If finalized, these amendments require designated employees of covered agencies (including the Southern California Regional Rail Authority and California Department of Fair Employment and Housing) to file Form 700 (Statement of Economic Interests) disclosures. If the workspace or its principals hold positions at designated agencies or have contractual relationships that place them in a "designated employee" category under an amended code, the registration obligation falls on those individuals, not on the freight forwarder entity itself. Scope: state and multi-county California jurisdictions specified in each code amendment.

**Title 13 and Title 15 proposed actions:** The DMV Driver Safety Hearings and CDCR hiring regulations, if finalized, would require operational reporting updates (e.g., driver qualification files, vendor certifications) rather than formal jurisdictional registration. No published data fields or form numbers are identified in the January 2024 register table of contents; these would appear in the full text of the individual Notice of Proposed Action, accessible via the OAL PDF archive or by contacting the Reference Attorney at staff@oal.ca.gov or (916) 323-6815.

To collect data fields relevant to any regulation that later becomes final, the workspace should monitor OAL's "Regulations Recently Filed with the Secretary of State" page, which lists effective dates and docket numbers for finalized rules.

*Source: California Regulatory Notice Register 2024, Number 1-Z, OAL, January 5, 2024. https://oal.ca.gov/2024/01/05/january-2024-california-regulatory-notice-registers/ | Regulations Recently Filed With The Secretary of State, OAL. https://oal.ca.gov/regulations_recently_filed_with_the_sos/*
